items.misconception: 14, because you read and calculate left to right (3 + 4 = 7, then 7 x 2).
items.random_wrong: 9, because you add all the numbers and then subtract the operator count.
items.why: Students compute left-to-right by default, ignoring the precedence rules in BIDMAS/BODMAS.

items.correct: 18 — division and multiplication share the same precedence; work left to right (6 x 3).
items.misconception: 2, because in BIDMAS multiplication comes before division (12 / 6).
items.random_wrong: 7, because you do 12 / 2 = 6 and then add 3 with the multiplication symbol meaning 'and'.
items.why: Students treat BIDMAS as a strict order (M before D), missing that multiplication and division have equal priority and are evaluated left to right.

items.q: What is the difference between area and perimeter?
items.correct: Perimeter is the total distance around the outside; area is the amount of surface inside (measured in square units).
items.misconception: Area and perimeter are different names for the same measurement.
items.random_wrong: Area is half the perimeter for any shape.
items.why: Students confuse the two terms because both describe the size of a shape, but they don't see that one is one-dimensional (length) and the other two-dimensional (length squared).

items.q: Volume of a cube with side 3 cm?
items.why: It's 27 cm³. “9 cm³” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The real figure is 120. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
